assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9.
The intragroup dividend has created a recognised intragroup monetary receivable exposed to foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk affecting profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a separate top-level hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The provided context includes the distinct IFRS 9 / IFRIC 16 hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation.
